goal: Reach at least 85% score on the exam.
constraints: Study schedule constrained to about 18 hours per week: roughly 2 hours on weekdays and 4 hours per day on weekends
Block length: ~50-minute focus blocks with ~10-minute breaks
Allow short standing intervals between 50-minute blocks (15-20 minutes) if feasible.

Sleep disruption management: neighbor's dog waking me early; adjust study schedule or sleep hygiene practices to protect sleep quality and maintain study focus (e.g., shift heavier blocks to later in the day if needed).
Contingency snack plan for cafeteria variability to minimize disruptions to study blocks.

Private tutoring viability: cost and scheduling may be prohibitive; explore low-cost alternatives and scheduling-friendly options (peer tutoring, TA office hours, study groups, free online resources).
decisions: Adopt active recall and spaced practice as the primary study strategy (problem-solving and retrieval over passive rereading).
Use 50-minute focus blocks with short breaks for study sessions.
Re-analyze the previous practice exam by topic and error type and convert each error into a 1–2 line SRS question.
Prioritize the top 2-3 weakest topics and allocate extra retrieval blocks to them each week.
Increase exam simulations: maintain one full timed practice exam every weekend and add one half-length timed set mid-week to practice pacing and error correction.
Maintain daily 50-minute blocks: one block closed-book problem-solving on a targeted weak topic, one block timed MCQs/speed drills with immediate error-driven remediation.
Use your error log as the spine of spaced practice — review cards on a 1-3-7-14 day cadence and force yourself to re-solve missed problems closed-book.
Set milestones: aim for 75-78% on a timed full test within 7-10 days, 80-83% by halfway through the plan, and consistent >=85% on at least two full tests in the final two weeks.
If helpful, convert these adjustments into a revised day-by-day schedule (50-minute blocks) for the coming weeks.
Adopt a two-week A/B test to evaluate ambient playlist effectiveness on focus, keeping caffeine constant.
Pack preferred snacks/meals to avoid cafeteria dependency.
entities: exam domain: probability and inference
timeframe: ~6 weeks
exam format: mostly MCQ with short calculation questions
study hours: weekdays ~2h, weekend days ~4h, total ~18h/week
resources available: course textbook, lecture notes, old practice exams
learning style: active recall and spaced practice
block length: 50 minutes per focus block (with ~10-minute breaks)
error log generation and usage for spaced practice
SRS questions generated from errors (1-2 lines each)
targeted weak-topic retrieval blocks weekly
assessment schedule: weekly full timed practice exam plus mid-week half-length timed set
review cadence: 1-3-7-14 day spaced repetition
milestones: 75-78% in 7-10 days, 80-83% by halfway, >=85% on two full tests in final two weeks
day-by-day schedule option: 50-minute blocks
noise management: potential use of noise-cancelling headphones to maintain focus during 50-minute blocks
focus aids: ambient playlist
caffeine influence on focus
test duration: two weeks
A/B testing for focus environment
snack management integrated with study schedule
baking as a reward mechanism in study plan
ergonomics: standing interval feasibility between study blocks
workspace optimization: desk layout and plant placement
cafeteria menu variability affecting snack availability
open: Identify covered subjects/topics and hardest topics
Confirm existing study materials (books, courses, question banks)
Note any constraints or preferred study times (work, family, time zones)
Plan and implement a two-week ambient playlist vs silence A/B test for focus blocks
Plan and implement a structured reward plan tying baking to study blocks
Provide quick snack options that minimize disruption to study time
Printer ink outage contingency: assess impact on planned printed materials and switch to digital/alternative resources; adjust today's plan if needed.
Gather seminar details (time, duration, recording availability) to decide attendance and impact on study blocks.
Plan and evaluate short standing intervals between 50-minute focus blocks as a feasibility adjustment
Assess impact of desk reorganization and plant placement on study focus; schedule a short adjustment session if needed.
Limit time spent in study community chat; establish boundaries (e.g., check-ins only during scheduled breaks) and use chat as a reward rather than during 50-minute focus blocks.
Address sleep disruption: identify practical steps (sleep hygiene, schedule adjustments) to minimize impact on study blocks.
ergonomics: newly assembled chair; evaluate its impact on comfort and posture during 50-minute focus blocks and adjust standing interval feasibility accordingly.
workspace optimization: chair upgrade may influence desk layout and plant placement; review and adjust as needed.
Assess impact of new chair on study flow and adjust routines to prevent loss of prime study hours
Assess viability of private tutoring (cost, scheduling) and identify low-cost alternatives (peer tutoring, TA office hours, study groups) to address potential barriers.
